mode.title: How do you want to plan this trip?
mode.subtitle: Pick one to get started.
mode.selfSubtitle: Build a trip step by step with Kippy
mode.agentSubtitle: Get professional help booking the trip

agent.title: Totally fair.
agent.para1: Kippy is built for self-organised group trips where one person (usually you) manages expenses on the fly. For a travel agent experience, you're better off in their hands.
agent.para2: That said, Kippy is still the best way to track and split expenses once the trip starts — even on agent-booked trips.
agent.orLabel: Or skip ahead and set up a basic trip:

tour.title: Great choice.
tour.para1: Group tours take care of the logistics. Kippy can still help your group track personal expenses, shared meals, and any extras you pick up along the way.
tour.para2: Search for tours and come back to set up an expense-tracking trip once you've booked.
tour.orLabel: Or set up a Kippy trip to track expenses:

dest.branchTitle: Do you have a destination in mind?
dest.ideasSubtitle: Help me find somewhere based on vibe
dest.inputSubtitle: City, country, or region — whatever you have in mind.

vibe.title: What's the vibe?
vibe.subtitle: Pick the feeling you're going for.

pace.title: How do you like to travel?
pace.subtitle: This shapes your pacing plan. You can always go off-script.
pace.packedSubtitle: See and do as much as possible. Every day is planned.
pace.balancedSubtitle: Mix of anchored plans and free exploration time.
pace.relaxedSubtitle: Mostly unplanned. Go where the mood takes you.

results.errorMsg: Could not create the trip. Please try again.

bali.blurb: Rice terraces, spiritual temples, and beaches for every mood. Cheap and welcoming year-round.

tulum.blurb: Crystal cenotes, eco-chic resorts, and the Riviera Maya's best-kept towns all within reach.

krabi.blurb: Island-hop between Koh Lanta and Koh Phi Phi. Snorkelling, climbing, or hammock time — your call.

queenstown.tagline: The adventure capital of the world
queenstown.blurb: Bungee jumping, skydiving, white-water rafting, and jaw-dropping fjords. You will not be bored.

patagonia.blurb: Hike Torres del Paine, glacier-hop in El Calafate, or kayak the channels of Tierra del Fuego.

nepal.blurb: Everest Base Camp or the Annapurna Circuit. The Himalayas will completely recalibrate your sense of scale.

tokyo.tagline: Sensory overload in the best way
tokyo.blurb: Ancient temples next to robot restaurants next to the world's best convenience stores. Works at every budget.

lisbon.blurb: Pastel buildings, world-class pastéis de nata, rooftop bars, and tram rides through history.

mexico_city.blurb: Frida Kahlo, unreal tacos, Aztec ruins, and a restaurant scene that rivals any European capital.

bologna.blurb: Birthplace of bolognese, mortadella, and tortellini. Every single meal is an event.

osaka.tagline: Eat your way through every block
osaka.blurb: Okonomiyaki, takoyaki, ramen wars, and the Dotonbori strip. Japan's most unfiltered food city.

san_sebastian.tagline: More Michelin stars per capita than anywhere on earth
san_sebastian.blurb: Pintxos bars that rival any tasting menu. Walk the old town and eat at every third door.

ibiza.blurb: Superclubs, legendary DJs, and daytime beach clubs. Hit it July–August for peak season energy.

bangkok.tagline: The city that genuinely never sleeps
bangkok.blurb: Rooftop bars at sunset, Khao San Road chaos at midnight, world's best street food hangover cure at 4am.

medellin.blurb: Cable car views, salsa dancing every night, and locals who genuinely want to show you around.

tips.beach_0: Book beach accommodation 3+ months ahead in peak season. Prices double and rooms disappear fast.
tips.beach_1: Check monsoon calendars before booking — SE Asian beaches are borderline unusable June–October.
tips.beach_2: Pack reef-safe sunscreen. Many marine areas now confiscate traditional SPF on entry.
tips.adventure_0: Book vaccinations 6–8 weeks before departure. Yellow fever, typhoid, and hep A are common for adventure routes.
tips.adventure_1: Get travel insurance that explicitly covers adventure activities — most standard policies exclude trekking and bungee.
tips.adventure_2: Download offline maps (Maps.me or AllTrails) before going remote. Signal is often non-existent on trail.
tips.city_0: Buy multi-day transit passes on arrival. They usually pay for themselves by day 2.
tips.city_1: Walk one block off the main tourist drag: prices drop by half, quality goes up.
tips.city_2: Group activities by neighbourhood — walk each area rather than criss-crossing the city all day.
tips.food_0: Eat at odd hours: 11am or 3pm, not peak tourist times. Lunch is frequently cheaper and better than dinner.
tips.food_1: Take a food tour on day 1 — you'll find places worth returning to for the rest of the week.
tips.food_2: Budget for one splurge meal. Food trips always have a non-negotiable worth saving for.
tips.party_0: Peak club hours rarely start before midnight. Rest until 10–11pm or you'll be exhausted before anything good happens.
tips.party_1: Bring more cash than you expect for covers, tips, and drinks. Card readers are slow in high-energy venues.
tips.party_2: Book a room with blackout curtains, or far from the noise. You need real sleep between nights out.

pacing.packed_short_headline_one: Up to {{total}} planned activities across {{days}} day
pacing.packed_short_headline_other: Up to {{total}} planned activities across {{days}} days
pacing.packed_short_body: {{days}} days goes fast. With up to {{maxPerDay}} anchors per day you can cover a lot without feeling rushed. Leave meals as exploration time rather than booking them in advance.
pacing.packed_mid_headline: ~{{total}} activities total — protect Day {{buffer}} as a buffer
pacing.packed_mid_body: Even on a packed schedule, protect one half-day with nothing fixed. Around day {{buffer}} is where trip fatigue tends to hit on {{days}}-day trips. Use it to re-visit somewhere, wander, or sit somewhere nice.
pacing.packed_mid_callout: Day {{buffer}}: keep this open — it's your fatigue safety valve.
pacing.packed_long_body: For trips over a week, plan in blocks rather than day-by-day. Set {{maxPerDay}} anchors per day for the first half, but leave the second half more open. You'll have real intel once you're there.
pacing.packed_long_callout: Build in 1 full rest day per week of travel.
pacing.balanced_short_body: Pick your {{total}} must-dos and let the rest happen organically. The anchor method: book one activity in the morning and one in the afternoon, then see where the evening takes you.
pacing.balanced_mid_headline: ~{{total}} anchors + a rest day on Day {{restDay}}
pacing.balanced_mid_body: This is the anchor method's sweet spot. Plan up to {{maxPerDay}} activities per day — morning and afternoon anchors — and leave evenings open. Day {{restDay}} is the classic fatigue point on {{days}}-day trips: protect it with zero planned anchors.
pacing.balanced_mid_callout: Day {{restDay}}: no anchors. Walk somewhere new, sit in a café, revisit a favourite spot.
pacing.balanced_long_body: For {{days}} days, plan {{maxPerDay}} anchors per day for most of the trip but protect Days {{r1}} and {{r2}} as rest days. You'll enjoy the planned days more when you're actually rested.
pacing.relaxed_short_body: One anchor per day, maximum. Book the one thing you'd regret missing and leave everything else open. This is how you find the hidden café, the random festival, the view from the wrong turn.
pacing.relaxed_mid_headline: {{total}} loose anchors — the rest is serendipity
pacing.relaxed_mid_body: One anchor per day keeps the trip shaped without being controlled. Use mornings to decide what that day's anchor will be based on how you feel. On {{days}} days you have real runway to adjust.
pacing.relaxed_mid_callout: Aim for at least 2 completely unplanned days — decide at breakfast what to do.
pacing.relaxed_long_headline: ~{{total}} loose anchors — plan as you go
pacing.relaxed_long_body: With {{days}} days at a relaxed pace, pre-book only what genuinely can't be done last-minute: popular hikes with permits, peak-season accommodation. Everything else is better discovered in motion.
pacing.relaxed_long_callout: Consider booking only the first 2–3 nights. Leave the rest flexible.
